title: Encounter "Invalid argument" error when chmod of a file (socket file) that created by socat
body:
### Issue Description

Hi, I have a podman machine (ARM type) that running on MacOS M1 chip. I encountered error chmod: changing permissions of 'somefile.sock': Invalid argument when I tried to change the permission of the file that I created by socat.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Step to reproduce:

1.  Start up the VM in either rootless or rootful mode (I tried both)
2. podman machine ssh
3. Run this command under the directory "/Users/someuser/somesubdir": "sudo socat UNIX-LISTEN:somefile.sock,fork,reuseaddr,unlink-early,user=core TCP:127.0.0.1:44444 &"
4. Under that directory, do "sudo chmod 755 somefile.sock"
5. Encounter "chmod: changing permissions of 'somefile.sock': Invalid argument"
6. Please note that the "/Users/someuser/somesubdir" is a directory I mounted to the VM. I gave the RW permission already.
7. However, if I execute the same "chmod" command on the host (MacOS), I could do it without error.


### Describe the results you received

Encounter "chmod: changing permissions of 'somefile.sock': Invalid argument" when running the chmod command in the VM

### Describe the results you expected

The chmod should complete successfully.

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
